slots.2_caller_identity: I review exotic and luxury car rental sites for a living
slots.3_target_buyer: Owner-operator, GM, or sales lead at exotic/luxury car rental company
slots.4_who_answers: Receptionist, owner (small shops), booking team, sales lead
slots.5_dollar_anchor: $5,000 to $15,000 a week in booked rentals
slots.5_anchor_math: 1-3 missed weekend bookings/wk × $2,500-$5,000 avg = $5K-$15K/wk for smaller operators (1-3 cars). Source: Ferrari/Lambo day rates $900-$2,500 (rodeoexoticrentals.com, monzaexotics.com).
slots.6_cpc_range: $1.50 to $7 per click ($14 near-me top end in Miami/NYC)
9_banned_terms: Turo (as competitor name on-call — use 'the next ad' or 'the next competitor')
Miner-style apology hedges (appreciate you picking up, out of the blue, etc.)
Slang permission (got a sec, cool?, Quick on me)
CTA (use 'booking button' / 'the button to book' / 'how they book')
hero / hero copy (use 'top of your page' / 'first thing they see')
above the fold (use 'top of the page')
TTI / LCP / Core Web Vitals (use 'load time' / 'how long the page takes')
developer / dev work / dev (frame fixes as fast + us doing them, not them hiring someone)
weekend of dev work (use 'quick fix' / 'I'd handle that in a day' / 'one of the fast wins')
afternoon work for a developer (use 'fast fix' / 'one of the quick wins')

universal_voice_locks: No {company} in beat copy — use 'your site'
Dual-path close (booked call OR email sample)

verbatim_site_language.five_second_mobile: Five-second mobile load. They book the next listing in three.
verbatim_site_language.luxury_renter_visual: A luxury renter taps the link, counts to three, opens the next ad.
verbatim_site_language.32_percent_stat: Every one-second delay on mobile cuts conversions roughly 32%.
verbatim_site_language.audit_format: Thirty minutes. Your live site on screen.
verbatim_site_language.differentiator: Every leak named in plain English no agency would dare use.
verbatim_site_language.yours_to_keep: Yours to keep whether you hire me to ship it or not.
verbatim_site_language.90_day_plan: Sequenced 90-day plan, ranked by conversion impact and effort to ship.
verbatim_site_language.tiered_wins: Week-one quick wins, 30-day medium lifts, 90-day rebuilds.

A.name: The CTA Leak
A.hook: Lead with the universal weakness — their call-to-action is doing the opposite of what it should
A.data_backing: 92% of audited prospects (46/50) have weak or moderate CTAs
A.best_for: Operators running ads — the weak CTA wastes every click

B.name: Next Ad Steals It
B.hook: A luxury renter taps your ad, counts to three, opens the next ad
B.data_backing: Verbatim from conversionexotics.com + universal mobile-load gap
B.lever: Status loss + FOMO + competitor frame (without naming names)
B.best_for: Operators losing share to peer-to-peer marketplaces and competitor ads

C.name: The 3-Issue Reveal
C.hook: I pulled up your site and saw three issues — the worst one is invisible to most operators
C.data_backing: CTA (92%) + Form friction (58%) + Hero/Trust (42-44%) — all data-backed
C.lever: Curiosity gap + loss aversion — hold the worst issue back for the audit
C.best_for: Operators with 'we just redesigned' objection-prone responses

beat_skeleton.2_PROBLEM: 17-24s — industry context, ends with question they can't answer
beat_skeleton.5_AUDIT_OFFER: 33s — $3K audit, free, 30 min shared screen, minute-20 decision
beat_skeleton.6_CLOSE: 30s — Thursday 10 or Friday 2, OR sample audit by email
